qa.user_verbatim_complaint: 修复 hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh 复合命令绕过 — Single ls/stat/cat reading protected path 仍然 ALLOW; compound `ls <flag> ; <write>` / `&&` / `||` / `|` / `$(...)` / backtick + write 必须 DENY (exit 2). 扩展或新建 test_AC_04_d4e6f8a0c3b5d7ea.py 覆盖复合命令旁路。
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: QA reproduced the EXACT Cycle-5 bypass payload `ls /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-testsid.flag ; python3 -c 'open("/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-testsid.flag","w").write("y")'` via subprocess invocation of hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh. Before this cycle: rc=0 (bypass succeeded — bug exhibitor). After this cycle: rc=2 AND stable stderr token 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' PRESENT. The exact payload the user complained about is now correctly DENIED. Independent QA matrix verification: 9/9 ALLOW cases rc=0 token-absent; 24/24 DENY cases rc=2 token-present. Sibling-file pytest: 70 passed, 2 skipped (env-gated), rc=0.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: Direct file inspection + git diff against baseline_head_sha 4d8b57d; matches dev.git_rationale root_cause_commit lineage from cycle 20260524-205206

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex adversarial review (QA Phase 11) reproduced a REAL in-scope bypass NOT covered by the 33-row matrix: ANSI-C quoting `$'-f''print'` defeats the find-action grep regex. Empirically verified by QA: `find /tmp $'-max''depth' 0 $'-f''print' /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-testsid.flag` → rc=0 token=False (Bash expands to `find /tmp -maxdepth 0 -fprint <flag>` which writes the flag). Also reproduced for `$'-de''lete'`, `$'-ex''ec' touch {} +`, `-f\print` backslash-escape form. This is the SAME attack class as Cycle-5 (allowlisted read verb prefix `find` + write surface) — the dev implementation closed the literal-token form but not the quote-concatenation form. PROPOSED_FIX (codex): require strict safe-read shape for find (only `find <literal-protected-path>` with optional whitespace), OR use shell-aware argv normalization (e.g., python shlex.split + scan tokens) instead of grep over raw text.
out_of_scope_observations.blocks_close_rationale: (a) The user's verbatim complaint payload (`ls <flag> ; python3 -c '...'`) is CLOSED. (b) The 33-row AC matrix is fully PASS — dev satisfied the literal AC contract. (c) The BA M1 enumeration listed find write-action tokens by name (-exec/-delete/-fprint*) but did not enumerate ANSI-C quote-concatenation evasion in the threat model; this is a BA spec gap, not a dev gap. (d) Iter-2 codex finding #1 (env-var entry-gate bypass) was similarly deferred out_of_scope per user spec. Recommend: surface to user as 'codex also found a real ANSI-C-quoted find bypass — separate cycle?' per codex Rule 3.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex (and QA reproduced) over-blocking on quoted metachars and shell-keyword words inside read commands. Verified denials of legitimate read flows: `grep -E 'foo|bar' <flag>` rc=2; `jq '.items[] | select(.kind == "bulk")' <sentinel>` rc=2; `jq '.ttl > 0' <sentinel>` rc=2; `grep ';' <flag>` rc=2; `grep then|done|if <flag>` rc=2. The grep-over-raw-text detector does not distinguish syntactically-active metachars from those inside single-quoted argument strings. PROPOSED_FIX (codex): make compound detection quote-aware (shlex.split + scan unquoted tokens only). Non-blocking because (a) the 33-row matrix has no ALLOW case for quoted-regex/quoted-jq reads — dev passed the literal AC, (b) over-blocking is safe-side (denies legitimate diagnostic flow but doesn't enable a write), (c) the user's verbatim AC examples are `ls`/`stat`/`cat` only.

qa.overall_assessment: PASS for cycle scope. Cycle-5 BLOCKER (exact user-verbatim payload `ls <flag> ; python3 -c '...write...'`) is verifiably CLOSED: subprocess invocation returns rc=2 with stable token 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' present. All 33 AC-01 matrix rows verified by QA independent subprocess invocation: 9 ALLOW rc=0 token-absent, 24 DENY rc=2 token-present (18 real-bypass exhibitors transitioned rc=0→rc=2 as required; 6 regression-guards preserved rc=2; 9 ALLOW preserved rc=0). AC-02 pytest invocation: 70 passed, 2 skipped (env-gated recursion guard), rc=0. Dev claim of 70/70 confirmed. Codex adversarial review surfaced 2 in-scope findings recorded as out_of_scope_observations: (1) ANSI-C-quoted find write-action bypass (same Cycle-5 attack class, missed by matrix) — RECOMMEND FOLLOW-UP CYCLE; (2) over-blocking of quoted-metachar read commands (regression risk for diagnostic reads). Neither blocks closure of THIS cycle's literal AC scope but the orchestrator must surface finding (1) to the user before treating the bulk-commit auth-flag contract as fully sealed.

root_cause_verification.rationale: Dev's git_rationale correctly identifies the root cause: prior-cycle Layer 1.F allowlist branch (b) was PREFIX-anchored read with redirect-only veto, missing non-redirect write channels (python -c, here-docs, process substitution, etc.) when chained after a read prefix. Iter-3 fix inverts the predicate from PREFIX-read to WHOLE-COMMAND-read by asserting (i) trimmed command STARTS WITH read verb, (ii) NO shell control structure / NO redirect / NO recursive shell / NO assignment / NO xargs / NO find write-surface flag anywhere. Diff inspection confirms the implementation matches the predicate; 33-row matrix confirms all enumerated attack shapes are denied; pure-read ALLOW preserved.

self_verification.notes: Every claim backed by either subprocess test result, file inspection, pytest output, or git diff. No claim relies on code-reading-only or assumption. The 2 codex findings are recorded as out_of_scope_observations with explicit blocks_close: false rationale + PROPOSED_FIX citations for follow-up.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5 xhigh) returned 2 ISSUEs (both with PROPOSED_FIX) + 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY confirming the exact Cycle-5 payload IS closed by the new semicolon detection. ISSUE #1: ANSI-C quoting `$'-f''print'` bypasses find write-action veto (raw-text grep can't see Bash word-concatenation) — same Cycle-5 attack class, missed by matrix. QA empirically reproduced via /tmp/qa-codex-issue1.py: 3 ANSI-C forms + 1 backslash-escape form all rc=0 token=False; literal control form correctly rc=2. ISSUE #2: Quote-naive compound detection denies legitimate `grep -E 'foo|bar' <flag>`, `jq '.x|y' <sentinel>`, `grep then <flag>`, etc. — over-block regression risk. QA empirically reproduced via /tmp/qa-codex-issue2.py: 7 legitimate read commands all rc=2.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Both findings classified per Codex Rule 3 (caller filters): ISSUE #1 = `in_scope_real_bug` for broader user requirement (Cycle-5 attack class) but `out_of_scope` for THIS cycle's literal AC matrix (BA M1 enumeration listed find write-action tokens by name, did not enumerate ANSI-C quote-concat evasion in the threat model — this is a BA spec gap, NOT a dev gap). Recommend orchestrator surface to user as 'codex also found a real ANSI-C-quoted find bypass — separate cycle?'. ISSUE #2 = over-block regression risk, recorded as observation but non-blocking (matrix has no quoted-grep ALLOW case; user's verbatim AC examples are ls/stat/cat only; over-blocking is safe-side). Iter-2 precedent: codex iter-2's env-var entry-gate finding was similarly deferred. Cycle-3 verdict: PASS for cycle scope. Following Codex Rule 4: requirement wins — but the cycle scope's requirement (close the literal Cycle-5 payload + 33-row matrix) IS met.

summary.followup_recommendation: RECOMMEND the orchestrator surface codex out_of_scope_observation #1 (ANSI-C-quoted find bypass) to the user before treating the bulk-commit auth-flag contract as fully sealed. The literal Cycle-5 payload is closed and the AC matrix is PASS, but a same-class attack remains exploitable. Per Codex Rule 3, this is information for the user/orchestrator decision — not a QA veto.
